To validate these theoretical properties, computational experiments are conducted using Python implementations for unit quaternions representation, with rotation matrices included as a reference representation to support empirical validation, examining numerical stability and computational efficiency.

title: Penerapan Dot Product dan Cosine Similarity dalam Analisis Kemiripan Pergerakan Harga Saham
abstract: Makalah ini membahas cara menggunakan representasi vektor dan cosine similarity untuk melihat seberapa mirip pergerakan harga saham yang satu dengan yang lain. Jadi, pergerakan harga saham itu kita jadikan seperti vektor di ruang Euclidean. Setiap bagian vektor mewakili return atau persentase perubahan harga saham dalam periode waktu tertentu. Untuk mengukur tingkat kemiripannya, kita pakai cosine similarity, yang pada dasarnya berasal dari konsep dot product. Yang menarik dari pendekatan ini adalah ia lebih fokus pada arah pergerakan harga, bukan pada besaran nilai harga itu sendiri. Dengan begitu, kita bisa membandingkan saham - saham yang harganya beda jauh skalanya tanpa masalah. Dari hasil analisisnya nanti, akan terlihat bahwa saham yang pola pergerakannya mirip biasanya punya nilai cosine similarity mendekati 1. Sebaliknya, kalau polanya berlawanan, misalnya yang satu naik terus yang lain turun, nilainya bisa jadi negatif. Konsep-konsep dasar dari aljabar linier dan geometri dapat kita gunakan untuk memahami dan menganalisis pola harga saham di pasar saham, berguna untuk mengejar saham yang belum naik yang mempunyai pola yang mirip dengan saham yang sudah naik.

title: Segmentation of Brain Tumor Regions in MRI Images Using Graph Laplacian–Based Spectral Clustering
abstract: Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is the primary non -invasive method to visualize internal body condition such as brain tumors. However, automating tumor detection is a difficult area of research due to tumors not forming well -defined objects with their unclear boundaries and appearance like healthy tissues. This paper attempts to view effectiveness of spectral clustering in image segmentation of brain tumors.

title: Optimization of Traffic Management Using Principal Component Analysis For Traffic Flow Surge Mitigation
abstract: Traffic surges are a common phenomenon in road networks, especially during peak travel periods such as national holidays and the homecoming period, making them a seasonal problem. These surges often occur not only on a single road segment but also on other parts of the network. This paper proposes a matrix -based analysis approach to identify dominant traffic flow patterns using Principal Component Analysis (PCA) implemented through Singular Value Decomposition (SVD). Traffic flow data is represented as a ti me–road segment matrix to extract dominant spatio - temporal patterns. The explained variance ratio of the first principal component is used as a quantitative indicator to distinguish local, corridor -based, or systemic traffic congestion at the network level. The implementation is carried out using a Python program and tested on several dummy data scenarios with different synchronization levels. The test results show that the proposed method captures the overall dominant traffic -flow structure and identifies road segments that significantly contribute to congestion. This approach provides simple, easily interpretable mathematical foundations to support data -driven traffic flow management and surge-mitigation strategies.

title: Analysis of Homography Transformation Application in Document Scanning Software
abstract: Document scanning software is a type of application, commonly found on smartphones, that enables users to convert physical documents into digital formats. This paper analyzes the role of linear algebra in document scanning software, with particular emphasis on homography transformation for perspective correction. The document scanning process is examined through a classical computer vision pipeline, including grayscale conversion, edge detection, morphological operations, contour detection, and quadrilateral selection. Once the document boundary is identified, homogeneous coordinates and singular value decomposition (SVD) are used to compute a homography matrix that maps the detected document plane to a rectangular output image. The resulting transformation enables geometric correction of perspective distortion through inverse image warping.

title: Penggunaan Latent Semantic Analysis (LSA) berbasis Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) dalam Rekomendasi Parfum
abstract: Parfum adalah salah satu cara seseorang mengekspresikan dirinya. Untuk mengenal kualitas suatu parfum, seseorang biasanya harus me rasakan wanginya secara langsung atau mencar i rekomendasi kesumber yan g relevan. Rekomendasi parfum dapat diterapkan dengan Teknik Latent Semantic Analysis (LSA) pada bahan baku dan deskripsi dari suatu parfum. Teknik ini menyajikan hasil yang mampu meli hat bahan baku dan deskripsi parfum secara menyeluruh.

title: Rank-Controlled Procedural Dungeon Generation Using SVD Implementation in Unity
abstract: Procedural Content Generation (PCG) is widely used in games to produce content algorithmically. A Rank-controlled dungeon generator is an

title: Analisis Pendekatan Vektor Euclidean dan Aljabar Geometri dalam Ray Tracing
abstract: Grafika komputer telah menjadi hal yang begitu esensial dalam kehidupan manusia di masa kini. Dalam beberapa tahun terakhir, bidang ini telah berkembang dengan sangat pesat, salah satunya adalah teknologi rendering yang mencakup ray tracing. Penelitian ini bertujuan untuk menganalisis konsep vektor Euclidean dan aljabar geometri yang diterapkan pada ray tracing untuk memahami cara kerjanya dalam menghasilkan suatu gambar. Pada penelitian ini metode yang digunakan adalah analisis matematis yang didukung oleh implementasi program sederhana. Hasil dari penelitian ini membuktikan bahwa konsep vektor Euclidean berlaku dan menjadi dasar da ri ray tracing , begitu juga dengan aljabar geometri.

title: Application of Eigenvalues in Movement Identification Using Accelerometer Data
abstract: This article discusses the application of eigenvalues in identifying movement based on accelerometer data. An triaxial accelerometer can measure acceleration in 3 perpendicular planes, X, Y, and Z. Those measurements might seem similar based on its magnitude and nothing can be interpreted from it. However, with the use of eigenvalues of the covariance matrix as compact descriptors, this article successfully builds a supervised machine learning for movement identification. The accelerometer data is first segmented using sliding window technique, and each window is labeled by majority voting. For every window, a 3×3 covariance matrix is computed from mean-centered acceleration samples, and eigenvalue features are extracted to summarize how the variance is distributed across principal directions. The extracted features are then standardized and reduced using Principal Component Analysis (PCA), before being classified using a Random Forest model. With window size = 128, step = 64, PCA components = 8, test size = 0.2, the model successfully achieved an accuracy of 1.0000 based on test split, while 5-fold cross-validation resulted in a mean accuracy of 0.9714 ± 0.0381. When applied to an unlabeled test sequence consisting of 2100 samples, the method produced 31 window predictions. These results indicate that covariance-eigenvalue descriptors, combined with PCA and Random Forest, are effective in identifying basic movements at the window level.

title: Pemisahan Background dan Foreground pada Video Menggunakan Low-Rank Approximation Berbasis Singular Value Decomposition (SVD)
abstract: Pada video dengan kamera statis, tampilan background umumnya tidak banyak berubah dari satu frame ke frame berikutnya, sedangkan objek yang bergerak hanya muncul pada sebagian kecil area gambar . Sifat ini dapat dimanfaatkan untuk memisahkan background dan foreground menggunakan pendekatan aljabar linier . Pada makalah ini, digunakan metode low-rank approximationberbasisSingular V alue Decomposition (SVD) untuk memodelkan background sebagai komponen berrank rendah dan foreground sebagai selisih antara video asli dan hasil aproksimasi background. Prosesnya dilakukan dengan mengambil beberapasingular valueterbesar digunakan untuk membentuk aproksimasi low-rank yang merepresentasikan background, sementara selisih antara data asli dan aproksimasi lowrank menghasilkan komponen foreground. Metode ini mampu memisahkan background dan objek bergerak secara cukup jelas dengan langkah komputasi yang relatif mudah dengan beberapa syarat teknis video yaitu kamera statis, background stabil, foreground jarang, dan cahaya stabil. Namun, karena foreground dimodelkan sebagai komponen residual akibatnya foreground yang dihasilkan tidak mempertahankan warna asli objek, melainkan hanya menunjukkan bagian yang mengalami perubahan.

title: Identification of NBA Most Valuable Player Candidates Using Singular Value Decomposition
abstract: The 2025 –2026 NBA season has started, and players are competing to become the Most Valuable Player (MVP). This study aims to identify strong MVP candidates using Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) applied to NBA player statistics. Player data, including points, rebounds, assists, steals, blocks, and other performance metrics, are organized into a matrix and normalized using Z -score standardization to ensure fair comparison across features. The matrix is then decomposed into U, S, and Vt matrices to reveal underlying patterns of player performance. By analyzing the contributions of eac h player to these patterns, a composite score is calculated to rank potential MVP candidates. Results using the 2024 –2025 season data show that top statistical performers, such as Nikola Jokić and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, align well with real -life MVP results, validating the method. Applying the same methodology to the 2025 –2026 season allows the identification of top candidates based purely on statistical performance. This study demonstrates that SVD is an effective, data-driven tool for evaluating player performance and highlighting potential MVP candidates, providing insights into both historical and current NBA seasons.

title: Analisis Quaternion-Based Gyroscope Control pada Gamepad untuk Aiming Real-Time di Game FPS

title: Extreme Rainfall Forecasting for Flood Risk Assessment in North Sumatra Using Singular Value Decomposition and LSTM-Based Deep Learning Models
abstract: North Sumatra is highly vulnerable to hydrometeorological disasters driven by extreme tropical rainfall, which remains challenging to predict using conventional numerical models. This study proposes a hybrid Deep Learning approach combining Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) and Long ShortTerm Memory (LSTM) to enhance forecasting accuracy and computational efficiency. SVD was employed to reduce the high dimensionality of 20 years of GPM IMERG satellite data (2005–2024), extracting dominant spatiotemporal climate modes. The LSTM network then modeled the temporal evolution of these features to project future anomalies. The model successfully predicted a ”Very High” rainfall intensity for November 2025, identifying critical flood hotspots in Medan and Sibolga. These projections are consistent with regional synoptic patterns observed in neighboring provinces and aligned with global atmospheric dynamics, specifically La Ni ˜na and Negative IOD conditions. The study concludes that the SVD-LSTM framework provides a robust and efficient tool for early flood risk assessment in complex tropical terrains.

title: Application of Line and Plane Intersection Geometry for Gamut Mapping in OKLab Vector Space

The abstract is to be in 9 -point, singlespaced type, and may be up to 8 cm long. Define all symbols used in the abstract. Do not cite references in the abstract. Do not delete the blank line immediately above the abstract; it sets the footnote at the bottom of this column. Leave two blank lines after the

title: Eigenmon : Application of a Modified LSA Algorithm to Measure Similarity of Pokémon Battling Capabilities
abstract: Being the highest -grossing media franchise of all time, Pokémon’s battling mechanics have a deeper complexity than they seem on the surface. The competitive scene behind these games has since determined a majority of each Pokemon’s roles in a battle and what their replacements could be. This paper aims to construct an algorithms to find out the similarities between a Pokemon’s battling capabilities by taking advantage of a modified Latent Semantic Analysis.

title: Self-Attention as the Core Prediction Algorithm in Large Language Models: A Linear Algebra and Geometric Perspective
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have become a central technology in modern natural language processing, enabling machines to generate coherent and context -aware text. At the core of these models lies the self -attention mechanism, which allows tokens within a sequence to dynamically interact and contribute to contextual representation. This work examines self-attention as the core prediction algorithm in LLMs from a linear algebraic and geometric perspective.

title: Modeling V ocaloid Style Synthesis Using Fast Fourier Transform
abstract: Digital Signal Processing (DSP) fundamentally relies on concepts from linear algebra, specifically the representation of signals as vectors in high-dimensional spaces. This paper presents a practical implementation of a spectral pitch-shifting algorithm to demonstrate these theoretical principles. By utilizing the Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT), we transform timedomain audio signals into frequency-domain vectors, allowing for direct algebraic manipulation of spectral components. The core methodology involves a linear coordinate mapping of frequency bins combined with linear interpolation to handle non-integer scaling factors. To validate the mathematical integrity of the algorithm, we conducted four distinct experiments: vector expansion (octave up), vector compression (octave down), a round-trip invertibility test, and a non-integer harmonic shift. The experimental results, analyzed through spectral density comparisons, confirm that discrete linear transformations can precisely alter pitch while maintaining signal structure. The successful reconstruction of the signal in the invertibility test ( T −1 ◦ T ≈ I) provides empirical evidence of the robustness of algebraic operations in audio synthesis.

title: Application of Singular Value Decomposition in Predicting Meta Champions and Win Conditions in League of Legends
abstract: This paper presents an application of Singular Value Decomposition in finding latent features between match data and champion data in the gameLeague of Legends. Match data and champion data are converted into a weighted matchchampion matrix, which is further processed using Singular Value Decomposition and Latent Semantic Analysis techniques to extract latent features. Cosine Similarity is used to find similarities between winning matches and champion drafts to draft queries. Based on these similarity scores, a recommendation system is constructed to suggest champions that maximize the probability of winning. Ultimately, this paper aims to provide a simple application of SVD and LSA for games such asLeague of Legends.

title: Comparative Study of Supervised Learning Algorithms for Disease Detection Based on Symptom Descriptions: An Approach Using NLP and Linear Algebra
abstract: This study investigates multi-class disease classification based on patient symptoms using supervised machine learning. A synthetic healthcare dataset containing demographic attributes and textual symptom descriptions is utilized. The proposed pipeline applies text preprocessing, TF–IDF vectorization, optional Latent Semantic Analysis (LSA) for dimensionality reduction, and supervised classification. Support Vector Machine (SVM), Logistic Regression, and KNearest Neighbors (KNN) are evaluated using hyperparameter optimization with Optuna and 5-fold cross-validation. Model performance is assessed using accuracy, macro F1-score, training time, and confusion matrices. Results show that overall predictive performance remains limited due to overlapping symptom patterns across diseases. While LSA reduces computational cost, it does not consistently improve classification accuracy. Logistic Regression achieves the best balance between performance and efficiency. These findings highlight the challenges of symptom-based disease classification and the trade-offs between model complexity and scalability.

title: Geometry of Lattice-Based Cryptography
abstract: The advancement of quantum computing threatens classical public-key cryptographic systems based on integer factorization and discrete logarithm problems, which can be efficiently solved using Shor’s algorithm. This has motivated the development of post -quantum cryptography, among which lattice -based cryptography has emerged as a leading candidate and has been standardized by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST). Lattice-based cryptography relies on the hardness of geometric problems defined over lattices, such as the Shortest Vector Problem (SVP) and the Learning With Errors (LWE) problem, which are believed to remain intractable even for quantum computers. This p aper examines lattice -based cryptography from a geometric and linear algebraic perspective by introducing fundamental lattice concepts and presenting a two-dimensional lattice implementation. The geometric interpretation highlights how lattice structure co ntributes to post-quantum security.

title: Secure Medical Image Watermarking: Analysis of Hybrid DWT-SVD and Arnold Transform Frameworks
abstract: In the rapid growth of technology, many types of digital data can be easily modified across various sectors, including the healthcare sector. Nowadays, medical images such as MRI, CT, and X-ray scans can be transmitted over networks, and these images often contain Electronic Patient Records (EPR) that include patient identities, diagnoses, and other sensitive information. Such data may be falsified or altered, potentially leading to serious consequences for patients. Furthermore, modified medical data can b e exploited for personal or malicious purposes, such as insurance fraud, blackmail, or targeted attacks against high-profile individuals. This paper will discuss the role of watermarking in ensuring patient data remains secure and unmanipulated, even though the image undergoes processing or interference during transmission, using the Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) and Singular Value Decom position (SVD) approaches. DWT is used to convert the spatial domain image into the frequency domain band; the watermark will be hidden in the robust low -frequency approximation band. SVD is used to insert the watermark into a singular value, so that the watermark is difficult to remove but can still be extracted after a geometric attack. Furthermore, to maintain patient data confidentiality, the watermark is encrypted using Arnold Cat Map, an algorithm that randomizes the position of watermark pixels. The encrypted EMR will be embedded into grayscale medical images. The Peak Signal to Noise Ratio (PSNR) is used to measure the quality of the image, and Normalized Correlation (NC) is used to evaluate the watermark robustness after the image is subjected to compression and noise. The results were considered successful if the method produced a PSNR value above 40 dB, reflecting very little visual degradation, while maintaining NC values above 0.90, indicating that the extracted watermark remains highly similar to the original watermark even after being subjected to various attacks.

title: Simulasi Pergerakan Manipulator Robotik di Ruang Euclidean dengan Pendekatan Geometri Vektor Menggunakan Algoritma Cyclic Coordinate Descent
abstract: Invers Kinematika adalah sebuah konsep yang menyelesaikan masalah lengan robot dapat bergerak mencapai posisi target secara otomatis di ruang 3D. Makalah ini membahas penyelesaian masalah invers kinematika menggunakan aljabar vektor yang lebih efisien dibandingkan metode matriks konvensional. Makalah ini memanfaatkan algoritma Cyclic Coordinate Descent (CCD), untuk menghitung pergerakan setiap sendi secara iteratif melalui operasi dot product untuk menentukan sudut belok dan cross product untuk menentukan sumbu putaran lengan. Setelah mendapatkan hasil operasi vektor, proses perhitungan dilanjutkan dengan perhitungan mencari vektor rotasi dengan rumus Rotasi Rodrigues untuk menghasilkan pergerakan lengan yang tidak menyebabkan masalah Gimbal Lock. Makalah ini juga memberikan simulasi berbasis Python untuk mendemonstrasikan lengan robot yang mamp bergerak menuju target secara real-time.

title: Music Genre Classification Using Complex Fourier Transform, Singular Value Decomposition, and Machine Learning
abstract: Music streaming platforms require automated genre classification for content organization and recommendation. Deep learning methods achieve high accuracy (>82%) but suffer from computational costs, lack of interpretability, and massive data requirements. This paper proposes an efficient alternative integrating Complex Fourier Transform for phaseaware spectral analysis and Singular Value Decomposition for dimensionality reduction. Extracting the top 100 singular values from (2050 × 130) complex spectrograms achieves compression ratio exceeding 2600:1 while retaining over 99% of spectral variance. Experiments on the GTZAN benchmark (1000 samples, 10 genres) demonstrate 77.0% accuracy with 10× faster training and 50× smaller models than CNNs, while preserving phase information critical for genre discrimination. This work demonstrates that fundamental linear algebra concepts provide efficient, interpretable solutions for music information retrieval.

title: Analisis Spektrum Matriks Transformasi Antrean: Mengidentifikasi Titik Jenuh Wahana Universal’s Islands of Adventure Melalui Vektor Eigen
abstract: Manajemen antrean pada taman hiburan merupakan sistem kompleks yang melibatkan dinamika perpindahan pengunjung antarwahana secara terus-menerus. Data waktu tunggu yang diperoleh sering kali bersifat statis dan hanya memberikan gambaran sesaat, sehingga tidak mampu memprediksi titik jenuh sistem dalam jangka panjang. Penelitian ini bertujuan untuk memodelkan aliran pengunjung di Universal’s Islands of Adventure menggunakan prinsip aljabar linier melalui analisis spektrum matriks. Data waktu tunggu dari lima wahana utama ditransformasikan menjadi sebuah matriks transformasi yang merepresentasikan peluang perpindahan pengunjung dalam sistem tertutup. Dengan mencari nilai eigen dominan yang merepresentasikan kondisi keseimbangan atau status tetap, penelitian ini mengekstraksi vektor eigen terkait untuk menentukan distribusi stasioner pengunjung. Vektor eigen tersebut digunakan sebagai instrumen untuk mengidentifikasi wahana yang secara matematis menjadi titik jenuh atau pusat kepadatan utama dalam ekosistem taman hiburan. Hasil analisis menunjukkan bahwa pendekatan vektor eigen memberikan prediksi yang lebih akurat mengenai akumulasi kerumunan dibandingkan hanya mengandalkan observasi data waktu tunggu tunggal, sehingga dapat menjadi basis pengambilan keputusan bagi manajemen operasional taman hiburan.

title: Pemodelan Timbre dan Sintesis Suara Berbasis Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) dengan Implementasi Plugin Virtual Studio Technology (VST)
abstract: Timbre atau warna suara merupakan atribut perseptual kompleks yang berperan penting dalam membedakan karakter instrumen musik, meskipun memiliki nada dan tingkat kekerasan yang sama. Pemodelan timbre secara digital menjadi tantangan tersendiri karena melibatkan struktur spektral yang dinamis serta hubungan harmonik yang berubah terhadap waktu. Makalah ini mengusulkan pendekatan pemodelan dan sintesis timbre suara berbasis Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC) dan Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) yang diimplementasikan dalam sistem aplikasi berbasis web. Proses analisis diawali dengan ekstraksi fitur MFCC dari sinyal audio menggunakan Short -Time Fourier Transform (STFT) sebagai representasi selubung spektral global yang relevan secara perseptual. Matriks fitur MFCC tersebut kemudian didekomposisi menggunakan SVD untuk mereduksi dimensi data dan mengekstraksi komponen utama yang merepresentasikan karakteristik timbre dominan instrumen. Nilai singular yang dihasilkan dimanfaatkan sebagai parameter timbre tingkat tinggi pada pros es sintesis suara, yang dikombinasikan dengan estimasi frekuensi dasar dan struktur harmonik. Sistem diimplementasikan dengan arsitektur client –server, di mana proses analisis dan dekomposisi dilakukan pada backend, sementara visualisasi hasil analisis serta sintesis suara interaktif disediakan melalui frontend, termasuk fasilitas keyboard virtual. Hasil implementasi menunjukkan bahwa pendekatan yang diusulkan mampu merepresentasikan dan merekonstruksi karakter timbre instrumen secara konsisten pada berbaga i nada, serta memungkinkan manipulasi timbre secara efisien melalui parameter hasil dekomposisi SVD.

title: Integrating Eigenvector and Singular Value Decomposition Methods for Comprehensive UEFA Champions League Final Standings Prediction
abstract: The new UEFA Champions League format makes it difficult to rank teams because each team plays different opponents with different difficulty levels. This paper proposes a mathematical model using linear algebra to predict the final standings of the 2025/2026 season more accurately. We use a modified PageRank algorithm to measure team strength based on match results and the quality of opponents. We also use Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) to analyze play styles from various performance statistics. By combining these two methods, the model can simulate remaining matches and estimate the final points for each team. The results show that our approach provides a better and more objective assessment than just looking at the current points in the standings.

title: Quaternion-Based Detection and Correction of Human Posture for A Health Monitoring System
abstract: Poor human posture during daily activities such as studying, working, and prolonged smartphone usage can lead to musculoskeletal discomfort and long -term health problems. This paper proposes a quaternion -based method for detecting and correcting human posture using three - dimensional orientation representation. Human body segments are modeled as vectors in ℝ³, whose orientations are represented by unit quaternions to avoid singularities commonly found in Euler -angle-based methods. Postural deviation is detected through the computation of a relative quaternion between the measured posture and an ideal reference posture, providing both the axis and magnitude of deviation. A simulation -based experiment is conducted to evaluate posture deviation and orientation stability before and after correction. The results demonstrate that quaternion-based orientation analysis provides a stable, compact, and mathematically robust framework for posture detection and correction, making it suitable for simple and low-complexity health monitoring systems.

title: Application of SVD to Differentiate Agent Footstep Sounds Across Surface in Valorant
abstract: The acoustic environment in a tactical shooter games like Valorant is a critical component of gameplay, where footstep sounds provide vital information about agent identity and location. This paper explores the application of linear algebra techniques, specifically Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) within the framework of Principal Component Analysis (PCA), to differentiate between various agent footsteps and surface materials. We implement a machine learning pipeline that extracts Mel -Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC) from audio clips, reduces dimensionality using SVD-based PCA, and classifies the data using Support Vector Machines (SVM). The results demonstrate that SVD effectively isolates the most variance - significant features of audio signals, enabling high -accuracy classification of game agents and terrain materials.

student.name: I Gusti Ngurah Alit Dharma Yudha

title: Analisis dan Rekomendasi Metode Hybrid Singular Value Decomposition pada Kompresi Citra dengan Pembanding Baseline JPEG
abstract: Penelitian ini mengusulkan pendekatan kompresi citra hibrida yang mengintegrasikan Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) dengan pemilihan rank adaptif dan kompresi JPEG untuk mengatasi keterbatasan SVD konvensional. Pada kompresi berbasis SVD tradisional, penentuan nilai rank $k$ secara manual seringkali gagal menyeimbangkan kualitas visual dan target ukuran file secara efektif. Metode Hybrid SVD yang diusulkan mengotomatisasi pemilihan nilai $k$ dengan menerapkan dua kriteria utama: retensi energi (mempertahankan 98% energi citra) dan pengambangan derau (noise thresholding sebesar 1% dari nilai singular maksimum). Eksperimen dilakukan pada berbagai jenis citra, termasuk pemandangan RGB, pola terstruktur, dan citra grayscale, dengan membandingkan performa Fixed-k SVD, Hybrid SVD, dan Baseline JPEG. Hasil penelitian menunjukkan bahwa meskipun Baseline JPEG secara konsisten mencapai nilai SSIM dan PSNR tertinggi, metode Hybrid SVD memberikan mekanisme pemodelan struktural yang lebih kuat dan adaptif tanpa intervensi manual. Keunggulan pendekatan hibrida ini paling terlihat pada citra multisaluran (RGB), sementara pada format grayscale performanya setara dengan Fixed-k SVD. Studi ini mengonfirmasi efektivitas SVD adaptif sebagai aplikasi praktis Aljabar Linier dalam mengoptimalkan representasi data citra.

title: A Linear Algebraic and Geometry Approach to Artificial Intelligence (AI) Text Detection
abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) increasingly mimic human writing, distinguishing between human and AI-generated text has become a critical challenge for academic integrity. This paper introduces a transparent, geometric approach to AI detection rooted in linear algebra, utilizing two primary features derived from sentence embeddings: Effective Rank , which measures the intrinsic dimensionality of the semantic space, and Total Variance , which quantifies local semantic spread. We demonstrate that human text exhibits significantly higher geometric complexity than probability-constrained AI-generated.

title: Kuantifikasi Kemiripan Wajah Melalui Pendekatan Geometri Vektor dan Dekomposisi Nilai Singular (SVD)
abstract: Analisis kemiripan wajah (face similarity) merupakan tantangan klasik dalam visi komputer yang memiliki aplikasi luas, mulai dari biometrika forensik hingga pencarian silsilah keluarga. Penelitian ini bertujuan untuk mengkuantifikasi tingkat kemiripan geometris antar-individu secara objektif menggunakan pendekatan Aljabar Linier yang ketat. Wajah dimodelkan sebagai himpunan vektor fitur yang merepresentasikan 𝑣 ∈ 𝑅 136 koordinat dari 68 titik landmark anatomis. Tantangan dalam mengukur kemiripan ini adalah variabilitas ekstrinsik yakni perbedaan skala, rotasi, dan posisi wajah dapat diatasi dengan menerapkan algoritma Orthogonal Procrustes Analysis yang diselesaikan melalui Dekomposisi Nilai Singular (SVD) untuk menghilangkan distorsi jarak Euclidean yang tidak relevan. SVD digunakan untuk memfaktorisasi matriks korelasi silang antara dua vektor wajah, menghasilkan matriks rotasi optimal yang meminimalkan error penyelarasan. Selanjutnya, metrik kemiripan dikembangkan menggunakan Weighted Euclidean Distance dengan pembobotan adaptif, di mana memberikan prioritas pada fitur struktural (mata, hidung) yang lebih stabil dibandingkan fitur jaringan lunak (pipi, rahang). Hasil eksperimen pada dataset keluarga inti menunjukkan bahwa algoritma ini menunjukkan pemisahan skor yang konsisten antara subjek berkerabat dan non-kerabat, di mana subjek dengan hubungan darah menghasilkan skor kemiripan rata-rata di atas 82%, sedangkan subjek tanpa hubungan kekerabatan berada di sekitar 73%.

title: Spectral Stat Analysis in Genshin Impact Character Build Optimization Using Eigenvalue
abstract: Character build optimization in Genshin Impact involves many synergic statistics that make it difficult to determine which attributes are the most important and strongly affect build effectiveness. This paper applies eigenvalue-based spectral analysis to study some character builds in Genshin Impact through linear algebra approach. Each build is represented as a vector in a real vector space, and a covariance matrix for multiple character build. Eigenvalue decomposition is used to identify dominant directions of different stat combinations. The results show that most builds can be explained by eigenvalues indicating redundancy of the build itself. This study demonstrates how eigenvalues and eigenvectors can provide meaningful insight into complex game mechanics without trial-and-error.

title: Pemodelan Sistem Persamaan Linear untuk Menentukan Strategi Pencapaian IP Semester Mahasiswa
abstract: Sebagai mahasiswa, Indeks Prestasi (IP) yang optimal merupakan salah satu tujuan utama. Namun, mahasiswa sering kesulitan untuk menentukan besaran nilai minimum yang harus dicapai pada setiap komponen penilaian (seperti UTS dan UAS) untuk memenuhi target mereka, terutama ketika berbagai mata kuliah yang mereka ambil memiliki bobot SKS dan komponen penilaian yang berbeda. Makalah ini mengusulkan pendekatan matematis menggunakan Aljabar Linier untuk memodelkan masalah tersebut menjadi kombinasi liniear dari nilai akhir setiap mata kuliah dan bobot SKS. Selanjutnya, hubungan antara komponen penilaian dan nilai akhir dibentuk menjadi Sistem Persamaan Linear (SPL). Dengan menggunakan metode eliminasi Gauss-Jordan, model ini memberikan solusi eksak berupa nilai minimum yang harus dicapai mahasiswa dalam ujian mendatang.

title: Similarity-Based Hoax Detection for Indonesian News Using TF–IDF and Latent Semantic Analysis
abstract: The rapid spread of hoaxes and misleading information on digital platforms has increased the need for automatic methods to assist in identifying potentially false news. This paper proposes a similarity-based hoax detection system for Indonesian news articles using linear algebra techniques. News texts are represented using the Vector Space Model with Term Frequency –Inverse Document Frequency (TF –IDF) weighting. To capture latent semantic relationships and reduce dimensionality, Latent Semantic Analysis is applied through truncated Singular Value Decomposition. Classification is performed by measuring cosine similarity between input texts and a collection of labeled documents in latent space. To prevent overconfident predictions, an uncertainty mechanism is introduced, along with a trusted publisher fallback for ambiguous cases. Experimental results demonstrate that the system performs consistently for inputs with sufficient contextual informatio n, while appropriately signaling uncertainty for short or novel texts. The proposed approach highlights the applicability of linear algebra-based methods for similarity-driven hoax detection and information retrieval tasks.

title: From Mountains to Math: Applying Singular Value Decomposition to Minecraft Terrain Generation
abstract: In this paper, I explore how Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) can be used to analyze and process procedurally generated terrain heightmaps. While Perlin noise is great for creating natural landscapes, we often need to fix them up for things like custom maps or smoother gameplay. I show how SVD breaks down terrain into different patterns, acting like a filter specific to the map. My experiments on Minecraft-like maps measure how good the terrain looks after compression and smoothing. The results show that keeping just 20–50 components works really well, saving space while keeping the mountains and valleys looking right. It turns out SVD is a powerful tool for understanding and manipulating terrain data.

title: Penerapan Konsep Ruang Vektor Umum dalam Efisiensi Algoritma EnkripsiElliptic Curve Cryptography(ECC)
abstract: Makalah ini memberikan penjelasan mengenai perbandingan antara dua pendekatan kalkulasi dalam kriptografiElliptic Curve Cryptography(ECC). Ditunjukkan bagaimana pendekatan koordinat proyeksi berbobot memiliki waktu pemrosesan yang lebih efisien dibanding dengan pendekatan koordinat biasa, terutama pada kalkulasi dengan nilai yang besar. Ini menunjukkan mengapa banyak implementasi ECC di dunia nyata menggunakan pendekatan koordinat proyeksi berbobot.

title: Application of Polynomial Interpolation on Secret Sharing
abstract: This paper explores the application of polynomial interpolation in Shamir's Secret Sharing scheme. The method divides a secret into N shares where only k shares are needed for reconstruction. By encoding the secret as the constant term of a random polynomial of degree k -1 and distributing evaluated points as shares, the scheme ensures that fewer than k shares reveal no information. The implementation demonstrate secret sharing by solving the system of linear equations from the polynomial interpolation through a Python program. This approach provides a robust solution for distributed security systems by eliminating single points of failure while maintaining threshold -based reconstruction.

title: Analisis Prediksi Dinamika Populasi Menggunakan Model Markov Berbasis Diagonalisasi Matriks
abstract: Dinamika populasi merupakan fenomena yang penting untuk dianalisis karena memiliki peranan penting dalam berbagai bidang, seperti perencanaan sumber daya, perumusan kebijakan publik, serta upaya menjaga keberlanjutan lingkungan. Untuk memahami dan memprediksi perubahan populasi dari waktu ke waktu, diperlukan suatu model matematis yang mampu merepresentasikan transisi antar kondisi populasi secara sistematis. Salah satu pendekatan yang efektif adalah model rantai Markov, yang memodelkan evolusi populasi berdasarkan probabilitas perpindahan antar keadaan melalui suatu matriks transisi. Meskipun demikian, analisis prediksi populasi dalam jangka waktu panjang menuntut perhitungan pangkat matriks transisi, yang secara komputasional dapat menjadi rumit dan tidak efisien apabila dilakukan secara langsung. Oleh karena itu, makalah ini membahas analisis prediksi dinamika populasi menggunakan model Markov dengan pendekatan diagonalisasi matriks. Melalui proses diagonalisasi, matriks transisi direpresentasikan ke dalam bentuk diagonal yang memungkinkan perhitungan pangkat matriks dilakukan dengan lebih sederhana dan terstruktur. Pendekatan ini tidak hanya mempermudah proses komputasi, tetapi juga memberikan kerangka analitis yang lebih jelas dalam mengkaji perilaku popu lasi jangka panjang, termasuk kestabilan sistem dan kecenderungan menuju keadaan tunak (steady-state). Hasil analisis menunjukkan bahwa penggunaan diagonalisasi matriks dalam model Markov mampu meningkatkan efisiensi perhitungan sekaligus memperdalam pemah aman terhadap struktur matematis dan karakteristik dinamika populasi yang dimodelkan.

title: Application of Latent Semantic Analysis for Automatic Summarization
abstract: The exponential growth of digital information has led to a significant challenge in processing large -scale textual data, particularly in the domain of digital journalism. Automatic Text Summarization (ATS) emerges as a vital solution to address this inform ation overload. This paper proposes an extractive summarization approach using Latent Semantic Analysis (LSA), a framework grounded in linear algebra. The core of the methodology involves transforming textual data into a high-dimensional Document-Term Matrix weighted by Term Frequency -Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF), which is subsequently decomposed using Singular Value Decomposition (SVD). To enhance the quality of the summary, the Steinberger and Ježek scoring method is implemented, utilizing the Eucl idean norm of sentence vectors weighted by their singular values to identify latent thematic structures. Experimental results demonstrate that the LSA -based approach effectively captures the primary semantic dimensions of a document while filtering out thematic noise through singular value thresholding. The study concludes that SVD provides a mathematically rigorous and unsupervised tool for information condensation, offering a scalable alternative to frequency -based summarization methods

title: Konstruksi Ruang Laten Spesifik Domain menggunakan SVD Ko-okurensi untuk Deteksi Sinonim pada E-Commerce Fashion
abstract: Pesatnya pertumbuhan e-commerce saat ini berdampak pada ledakan volume data produk yang diiringi dengan tingginya variasi linguistik, khususnya pada domain fashion. Hal ini menimbulkan tantangan dalam sistem pencarian, terutama dalam penggunaan algoritma yang sering kali gagal mengenali sinonim atau istilah spesifik domain. Makalah ini mengusulkan konstruksi ruang laten menggunakan metode Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) yang diterapkan pada matriks ko-okurensi kata. Pendekatan ini bertujuan untuk mengekstraksi kedekatan semantik antar istilah guna meningkatkan akurasi deteksi sinonim pada katalog produk. Melalui transformasi ke ruang laten berdimensi rendah, hubungan semantik yang tersembunyi dapat dipetakan secara matematis. Hasil penelitian ini diharapkan dapat meningkatkan relevansi hasil pencarian pada platform e-commerce melalui pengenalan istilah yang lebih cerdas dan kontekstual

title: When Holiday Meets Linear Algebra: A Procedural Generation of Christmas Ornament Motifs
abstract: Christmas ornaments often rely on symmetric and repetitive geometric patterns, yet their design is commonly created through manual artistic processes. This paper presents a procedural ornament generation method based on linear algebra and Euclidean geometry. The ornament is modeled on a circular domain, where geometric motifs are constructed using vectors and distance fields, then replicated through rotation and reflection matrices to enforce dihedral symmetry. A multi layer superposition is applied to enhance visual complexity, while a simple shading model produces a glass-like appearance. The generation process is fully deterministic from a single scalar input, enabling reproducible yet diverse ornament designs.

title: Application of Low-Rank Approximation via Singular Value Decomposition for Detecting Synthetic Recoil Control in Valorant
abstract: Competitive first-person shooter (FPS) games such as Valorant are increasingly targeted by automated recoil control macros, which undermine fair play. I propose a non-intrusive approach using SVD to detect synthetic recoil control by analyzing mouse movement patterns. The key observation is that synthetic inputs repeating identical motions across trials have low-rank structure, unlike human motion which contains natural motor variability. When mouse trajectory data is decomposed via SVD, scripted behavior concentrates over 98% of variance in the first singular value, while human control shows more distributed energy across modes. This enables detection of aiming assists without system-level monitoring or behavioral profiling, demonstrating how linear algebra can address integrity concerns in competitive gaming.

title: Penerapan Metode Nonnegative Matrix Factorization (NMF) dalam Rekomendasi Penjualan Barang di e- commerce Berdasarkan Rating yang Diberikan Pengguna
abstract: E-commerce is one of many sec tor that has been on the rise in t his era o f information and technology. One important a spect in t he e-commerce process is the p rocess of giving recommendation in selling thi ngs to the users. Nonnegative Matrix Factorization (NMF) is one of the methods that can be used to do such thing. This method factorizes a nonnegative matri x into two smaller matrix t hat is also nonnegative. The program analysis resu lts show that this method is still far from perfection having many de ficiency in giving users their re commendation. Despite that imperfection, this method is one of the methods t hat can be easily implemented using concep ts of linier and geometric algebra while still being able to gi ve relevant recommendation to users based on the ratings that they give to products bought by them.

title: Optimization of a 3-DOF Robot Arm End-Effector in Handling Singularities Using the Damped Least Squares (DLS) and Quaternion Representation
abstract: In the field of computer graphics and robotics simulation, Procedural Locomotion has become a critical technique for generating realistic character movement in dynamic environments. Unlike static animation clips, procedural systems must adapt limb positioning in real-time to navigate uneven terrain. However, this adaptability introduces significant mathematical challenges, particularly Kinematic Singularity—a state where the manipulator loses degrees of freedom, causing numerical instability in standard Inverse Kinematics (IK) solvers. Furthermore, accurate foot alignment on sloped surfaces is often hindered by the Gimbal Lock phenomenon inherent in Euler angle representations. This paper presents a robust kinematic solution for a 3-DOF mechanical leg. We implement a Damped Least Squares (DLS) solver to ensure stable convergence near singular configurations and utilize Quaternion algebra for artifactfree orientation control. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method allows the manipulator to execute complex "step-down" maneuvers onto distant, low-elevation targets with high precision( 𝒆𝒓𝒓𝒐𝒓<𝟏𝟎ି𝟒 m) and smooth velocity profiles, effectively mitigating the instability issues common in real-time gait generation.

title: Brain Connectivity Analysis Using Graph Fourier Transform and Spectral Decomposition of EEG Networks
abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG) analysis using traditional channel-wise approaches overlooks the spatial structure of brain connectivity and the relationship between network topology and neural activity patterns. This paper proposes a Graph Fourier Transform (GFT) framework to analyze EEG-derived connectivity networks, modeling electrodes as graph nodes and connectivity strengths as edge weights. Graph Laplacian eigendecomposition distinguishes globally synchronized activity from localized patterns, while graph spectral features, such as spectral energy and entropy, provide interpretable measures of neural activity in relation to network topology. This approach offers a novel perspective on linking functional brain network structure with EEG dynamics.

title: Eigenvectors as Directions of Meaning in High-Dimensional Data: A Geometric Perspective for Drone State and Motion Analysis
abstract: This paper explores eigenvectors and eigenvalues from a geometric perspective, specifically how these concepts can be applied to analyze drone position data in 3D space. The core idea is simple: if we have a collection of data points forming a particular pattern (e.g., elongated in one direction), the eigenvectors of the covariance matrix will point toward that pattern. For drone analysis, the dominant eigenvector indicates the primary flight direction, while eigenvalues show how much variance exists in each direction. Using simulated linear and spiral trajectories, this paper demonstrates that eigenvectors can reconstruct flight direction with high accuracy (error around 1.42◦). This approach forms the mathematical foundation of Principal Component Analysis (PCA), widely used in machine learning and data analysis.

title: Simulasi Ikan dengan Animasi Prosedural
abstract: Animasi prosedural merupakan pendekatan komputasional yang menghasilkan gerakan dinamis melalui algoritma matematis, berbeda dengan animasi manual frame-byframe. Makalah ini mengeksplorasi implementasi konsep aljabar linear dan geometri dalam simulasi gerakan ikan yang realistis, mencakup kinematika vektor, rotasi dengan bilangan kompleks, analisis Jacobian untuk kinematika invers, dan analisis nilai eigen untuk mode getaran alami. Sistem yang dikembangkan menggunakan algoritma Reynolds Steering Behaviors dan metode iterasi tunggal dari Backward Reaching Inverse Kinematics (FABRIK) untukconstraintposisi. Hasil implementasi menunjukkan bahwa kombinasi persamaan gelombang berjalan dengan constraint kinematik menghasilkan gerakan berenang yang natural dan responsif. Analisis matematis melalui matriks kekakuan dan dekomposisi nilai eigen mengungkapkan modemode pembengkokan tubuh yang sesuai dengan pola gerakan ikan biologis. Perbandingan metode FABRIK dengan pendekatan Jacobian-based mendemonstrasikan kelebihan dan kekurangan antara kedua metode.

title: Color-Aware ASCII Art Generation using Eigenvalue Analysis of the Color Structure Tensor
abstract: Most ASCII art generators convert pixel brightness directly into character density. While effective for simple shading, this approach often fails to preserve structure, especially in regions where colors differ but brightness remains similar (isoluminant regions). In this study, we propose using the Color Structure Tensor to improve structural preservation. By treating the image as a 3D vector field in RGB space and analyzing the summed structure tensors ( SRGB = SR + SG + SB), we can detect ”chromatic edges” that standard grayscale operators miss. We use the dominant eigenvector to determine edge orientation, selecting characters like /, \, and | to align with the local color gradient. Our experiments demonstrate that this method recovers geometric boundaries in images with equal luminance, offering a clearer and more stylistically accurate representation of colored imagery.

title: NPC Gift Optimization Using Cosine Similarity Clustering and Eigenvector Centrality Ranking in Stardew Valley
abstract: Stardew Valley is an farming simulation game that features a gift-giving system to build relationships with nonplayer characters (NPCs). Each NPC has unique preferences for certain items, making gift-giving a strategic aspect of the game. This paper aims to analyze the gift preferences of NPCs using cosine similarity clustering and eigenvector centrality ranking to optimize gift-giving strategies. By constructing a preference matrix and applying hierarchical clustering, we group NPCs with similar preferences. We then compute the eigenvector centrality for items within each cluster to identify the most influential gifts. The results provide players with optimal gift recommendations, optimizing their playthrough.

title: Penerapan Eigenvalue Decomposition dan Power Iteration Method dalam Analisis Kerentanan Jaringan Drainase Perkotaan
abstract: Jaringan drainase perkotaan adalah salah satu elemen penting dalam mencegah banjir, namun cara analisisnya saat ini masih terbatas—hanya memeriksa komponen per komponen tanpa melihat bagaimana semua bagian terhubung dan saling memengaruhi. Makalah ini mengusulkan pendekatan baru menggunakan aljabar linear, khususnya eigenvalue decomposition dan power iteration method, untuk menemukan titik-titik kritis dalam jaringan drainase yang dimodelkan sebagai matriks Laplacian. Dengan menggabungkan analisis topologi jaringan (spectral centrality) dan parameter fisik seperti elevasi, kapasitas aliran, serta sedimentasi, penulis menganalisis jaringan drainase dummy dengan 200 nodes dan 851 edges. Hasil menunjukkan algebraic connectivityλ 2 = 0.075mengindikasikan jaringan sangat rentan terhadap pemisahan. Sekitar 30% titik termasuk kategori kerentanan tinggi, dan korelasi eigenvalue centrality terhadap vulnerability mencapair= 0.715, membuktikan metode ini efektif untuk menilai kerentanan sistem. Analisis sensitivitas bobot menunjukkan model robust terhadap variasi parameter moderat (Jaccard index≈1.0 di sekitar konfigurasi baseline), dan partisi spektral berhasil membagi jaringan menjadi zona pemeliharaan yang koheren. Metode ini dapat digunakan untuk memprioritaskan pemeliharaan preventif dan mengembangkan sistem monitoring berbasis waktu nyata.

title: Aplikasi Dekomposisi Nilai Singular (SVD) untuk Penentuan Koefisien Stoikiometri Reaksi Kimia Kompleks Melalui Analisis Ruang Nul
abstract: Dalam kimia, hukum kekekalan massa menyatakan bahwa jumlah atom dalam reaktan dan produk pada suatu reaksi kimia selalu sama. Penyataraan reaksi kimia, terutama pada reaksi redoks kompleks , sering menjadi tantangan jika dilakukan perhitungan secara manual. Secara matematis, masalah penyetaraan reaksi kimia dapat dipetakan menjadi Sistem Persamaan Linier (SPL) Homogen dengan mencari solusi non -trivial. Metode Eliminasi Gauss -Jordan rentan terhadap ketidakstabilan numerik, seperti ill-conditioned atau floating-point error . Dengan menerapkan metode Singular Value Decomposition (SVD), hasil perhitungan lebih tahan terhadap ketidakstabilan numerik tersebut. Makalah ini juga memiliki keluaran s ebuah perangkat lunak berbasis Python dikembangkan dengan fitur parsing string untuk memproses persamaan kimia secara langsung dan mengaplikasikan SVD untuk menentukan koefisien tiap molekulnya.

title: Camera Calibration and 3D Distance Estimation in Euclidean Space Using
abstract: This paper studies how linear algebra and Euclidean geometry can be used to estimate 3D distance from a depth camera. We use the Intel RealSense D455 as a case study. First, we review the pinhole camera model and the intrinsic camera matrix, which describe how 3D points are projected to 2D image pixels. Then we show how pixel coordinates and depth values can be converted back into 3D coordinates in the camera frame using vector and matrix operations. The distance from the camera to the object is obtained as the Euclidean norm of this 3D vector. We also discuss the idea of camera calibration as a linear system solved by least squares. This gives a simple mathematical framework that links depth sensing with core concepts from linear algebra.

title: Least Squares Approximation of ECG Waveforms for Cardiac Signal Analysis
abstract: ECG signal analysis is essential for cardiac diagnostics, yet raw data is frequently corrupted by environmental noise. This study implements a Least Squares (LS) approximation framework using Record 101 from the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database. Discrete signals were preprocessed with baseline removal, moving average smoothing, and Z-score normalization to ensure numerical stability. The approximation is formulated as an orthogonal projection of the signal vector onto a lowerdimensional polynomial subspace. While the global polynomial basis effectively tracks low-frequency morphological trends, the sharp QRS complexes remain as residuals due to the inherent smoothness of the basis functions. These findings confirm that LS derived metrics, such as the residualL 2 norm and polynomial coefficients, serve as reliable indicators for signal complexity and cardiac characterization.

title: SVD-Stabilized Perspective-n-Point Estimation for Vision-Based Relative Localization in UA V Formation Systems
abstract: Vision-based relative localization is a core capability in multi-UA V systems operating in GPS-denied environments. A common formulation of this task relies on the Perspective-n-Point (PnP) problem, where the relative pose between a camera and a known 3D structure is estimated from 2D image observations. Although numerous PnP solvers exist, their performance in practical UA V scenarios is often limited by numerical instability caused by image noise, unfavorable point configurations, and illconditioned linear systems. This paper studies the role of Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) as a stabilization mechanism in PnP estimation. By analyzing how SVD-based least-squares solutions and pseudoinverse formulations mitigate noise amplification and degeneracy, we provide a clear mathematical foundation for SVDstabilized PnP pipelines. The discussion is contextualized within vision-based relative localization for UA V formation systems, highlighting why numerical conditioning is critical for reliable pose estimation in real-world robotic applications.

title: Estimating Football Goal Probability from Single Images Using Wedge Product and Affine Basis Transformation
abstract: In modern football analytics, goal probability models usually rely on rich spatiotemporal tracking data, multiple synchronized cameras, or wearable sensors, which are expensive and inaccessible for amateur teams and casual analysis. This paper presents a system that estimates the probability of scoring a goal from a single still image of a shooting situation by combining concepts from geometric algebra and linear algebra. Perspective image coordinates are mapped to a metric two-dimensional pitch coordinate system through an affine basis transformation calibrated from the goal posts and ball position. Lateral distances with respect to the shooting line are computed directly using the wedge product as the signed area of a parallelogram, allowing the perpendicular distance to be obtained without explicit rotation matrices. The spatial configuration of defenders is modeled through covariance analysis and eigenvalue–eigenvector decomposition, which captures the dominant orientation of the defensive wall. The final goal probability is produced by a logistic regression model that incorporates distance, shooting angle, and a defensive pressure factor that accounts for both goalkeeper positioning and defender obstruction derived from eigen-analysis. The proposed approach demonstrates that single-image geometric reconstruction can approximate realistic goal probabilities while remaining consistent with the teaching material of Linear Algebra and Geometry.

title: Judul Tidak Terdeteksi
abstract: Sistem pengenalan wajah menghadapi tantangan signifikan dengan variasi pose (yaw, pitch, roll), di mana akurasi turun dari 95% (frontal) menjadi 70% pada rotasi 45°. Pendekatan berbasis Euler angles mengalami gimbal lock di singularitas ±90°, mengorbankan stabilitas numerik. Makalah ini mengusulkan normalisasi pose wajah berbasis aljabar quaternion yang bebas singularitas dan stabil. Tahapan penelitian mencakup melakukan estimasi landmark 3D, menentukan sudut pose kepala (φ, θ, ψ), mengkonversi ke unit quaternion q, dan menerapkan rotasi p' = q ⊗ p ⊗ q⁻¹ untuk normalisasi pose frontal. Validasi mencakup analisis akurasi rotasi dengan error rekonstruksi < 1e-10, perbandingan stabilitas numerik yang menunjukkan keunggulan quaternion dibanding matriks rotasi, serta evaluasi pengenalan wajah yang menunjukkan kenaikan akurasi dari 12.5% (tanpa normalisasi pose) menjadi 100% setelah dinormalisasi menggunakan quaternion. Hasil membuktikan efektivitas aljabar quaternion untuk rotasi 3D dalam computer vision.

student.name: Makalah If Aljabar Linier Dan Geometri Itb Semester I Tahun

title: Implementation of Reinhard Method Using Orthogonal Vector Basislαβfor Color Transfer: Analysis of Success and Failure Modes
abstract: This paper discusses the application of Linear Algebra concepts, especially change of basis and vector orthogonality, in digital image processing. The Reinhard method is used to transfer color statistics from a target image to a source image. The main focus of this research is to prove the effectiveness of the lαβcolor space which has orthogonal vector basis to minimize correlation between color channels, unlike the RGB space which is highly correlated. Through systematic experiments across nine source-target combinations, we analyze both successful transfers and failure modes, revealing that success depends critically on structural similarity, target color variation, and semantic compatibility. Experimental results show that transformation to orthogonal basis successfully decorrelates the data for most images (92-94% correlation reduction), but this mathematical correctness does not guarantee perceptually acceptable results when global statistics are applied to semantically complex scenes.

title: Accelerating High-Resolution Tumor Detection in MRI Scans using Fast Fourier Transform-based Convolutional Neural Network Manuel Thimoty Silalahi
abstract: Brain tumor detection utilizing Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is a of the most critical task in modern medical diagnostics that relies heavily on the performance of Machine Learning, like CNNs. While CNNs demonstrate superior accuracy, standard spatial convolution operations impose a quadratic computational complexity ofO(N 2K 2), creating a significant bottleneck, particularly with large kernel sizes. This paper analyzes the mathematical optimization of convolutional layers by transforming operations from the spatial domain to the frequency domain, also called as Discrete Fourier Transform. However, the time complexity taken to compute DFT in conventional way is still approximatelyO(N 2). Therefore, Fast Fourier Transform came and do its magic, so that the transformation can be done in onlyO(NlogN). This study also implements a CNN architecture from scratch using NumPy on a subset of the MRI Tumor dataset resized at a resolution of128×128. Theoretical and experimental analyses demonstrate that the FFT-based approach reduces complexity toO(N 2 logN), offering significant computational efficiency improvements over naive convolution methods, thereby validating the vital role of vector basis transformation in Deep Learning optimization.

title: Analisis Volatilitas Aset Keuangan sebagai Objek Geometris dalam Ruang Euclidean
abstract: Makalah ini mengusulkan interpretasi geometris terhadap volatilitas aset keuangan dengan memodelkan deret waktu return aset keuangan sebagai vektor dalam ruang Euclidean. Setiap dimensi merepresentasikan interval waktu diskrit. Dengan memanfaatkan formalisme norma vektor, dot product, dan sudut antar vektor, volatilitas diinterpretasikan sebagai panjang vektor return, sementara perubahan dinamika pasar dianalisis melalui pergeseran orientasi (arah) vektor antar periode. Pendekatan ini memungkinkan karakteri sasi risiko dan stabilitas pasar melalui atribut geometris murni, memberikan perspektif alternatif dalam memahami struktur data keuangan tanpa melibatkan pemodelan prediktif maupun pengukuran kemiripan antar aset.

title: Penerapan SVD dan Machine Learning untuk Sistem Pengenalan Suara pada Game Navigasi 2D Sederhana
abstract: Machine learning adalah cabang dari Artificial Inteligence yang memungkinkan p rogram u ntuk belajar dari data, mengidentifikasi pola, dan membuat keputusan dengan sendirinya tanpa arahan dari penggun anya. Sistem Pengenalan Suara atau Voice recognition adalah teknologi yang memungkinkan program untuk memproses u capan manusia menjadi p erintah yang d apat dimengerti oleh mesin. Makalah ini berfokus pada pengembangan game navigasi 2 dimensi yang pergeraka nnya d ikontrol melalui perintah suara. Dalam implementasinya, sinyal audio diubah menjadi representasi frekuensi menggunakan mel - spektrogram, kem udian direduksi menggunakan teknik Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) untuk m engekstraksi dan mereduksi dimensi fitur suara. Fitur yang telah direduksi kemudian diklasifikasikan menggunakan algoritma machine learning.

title: Enhancing Numerical Stability in High-Frequency Trading Algorithms: A Householder QR Decomposition Approach to Rolling Linear Regression
abstract: High-Frequency Trading (HFT) systems rely on fast, adaptive algorithms that frequently utilizes continuously updated statistical models to interpret market signals. However, the limit order book data utilized in these strategies often extreme multicollinearity, providing unstable numeric standard regression techniques. This paper investigates the critical role of numerical precision in HFT by comparing the Normal Equations against the Householder QR matrix decomposition within a rolling linear regression framework. This paper also provide a comprehensive theoretical foundation —covering matrix condition numbers, eigenvalues, and orthogonal transformations—to explain why the standard approach is vulnerable to an er ror in floating -point arithmetic.

title: A Linear Algebra Approach to Football Team Ranking in English Premier League Using the Massey Method and Matrix Operations
abstract: The accurate ranking of sports teams is a fundamental problem in analytics, often limited by traditional point -based standings that ignore strength of schedule and margin of victory. This paper presents a linear algebraic approach to ranking English Premier League teams for the 2023 -2024 season using the Massey Method. By modeling match outcomes as a system of linear equations where rating differences equate to goal differentials, we construct a least -squares problem to minimize prediction error. The study details the mathematical construction of the Massey Matrix ( M) and the resolution of its inherent singularity through boundary constraints ( 0ir= ). Using Python and NumPy to process the full 380 -game schedule, we derive continuous ratings that contrast with official standings. Results reveal significant discrepancies, highlighting the efficiency luck of Aston Villa and the statistical superiority of Newcastle United. Furthermore, the method offers a "pure" metric that corrects for administrative distortions, such as Everton's points deduction. This research bridges abstract linear algebra concepts, specifically vector spaces and matrix decomposition, with practical applications in data science.

title: Low-Rank SVD Approximation for Denoising Handwritten Touch Trajectories Used To Fulfill The “IF2120 – Linear Algebra and Geometry” Paper Assignment
abstract: Capturing handwriting on touchscreen /touchpad often results in shaky or jittered lines. This issue is usually caused by sensor limitations and natural hand tremors , all of which degrade the quality of digital strokes. This paper proposes a solution to smooth these trajectories using a data-driven technique known as Singular Spectrum Analysis (SSA). Instead of forcing the handwriting to fit a pre -set mathematical formula (parametric), our method analyzes the stroke data itself (nonparametric). By transforming the input into a Hankel Matrix and applying Singular Value Decomposition (SVD), we can effectively separate the user’s intended movement from the random noise. We achieve this by retaining only the strongest signal components (Low-Rank Approximation) while discarding the high -frequency jitter. Our experiments with both simple curves and complex cursive writing demonstrate that this approach is highly effective. It successfully eliminates the "staircase" artifacts common on touchpads without blurring out or losing important details, such as sharp turns or small loops in letters. The result is digital handwriting that looks smooth and natural.

title: Analisis Efektivitas Jalur Evakuasi Gunung Agung Menggunakan Konsep Ruang Vektor dan Proyeksi Ortogonal
abstract: Gunung Agung merupakan salah satu gunung berapi aktif di Indonesia yang berlokasi di Bali. Gunung ini menjadi gunung yang disakralkan oleh umat Hindu sehingga banyak upacara adat yang dilakukan di sekitar gunung, seperti Upacara Eka Dasa Rudra. Gunung Agung pun dipercaya sebagai istana para dewa sehingga didirikan Pura Agung Besakih di lereng gunung yang ditujukan sebagai tempat perantara para dewa. Oleh karena banyaknya aktivitas di sekitar gunung, terlebih gunung ini masih bersifat aktif, diperlukan jalur evakuasi yang efektif dalam meminimalkan risiko korban jiwa saat terjadi erupsi. Makalah ini dibuat dengan tujuan untuk menganalisis efektivitas jalur evakuasi Gunung Agung dengan menggunakan konsep -konsep yang telah diajarkan pada mata kuliah Aljabar Linier dan Geometri, khususnya konsep terkait ruang vektor, basis vektor, norma vektor, perkalian titik, dan proyeksi ortogonal. Jalur evakuasi direpresentasikan sebagai arah evakuasi dominan yang dimodelkan sebagai vektor pada ruang Euclidean, sedangkan arah aman didefinisikan sebagai vektor radial yang menjauhi pusat erupsi. Efektivitas rute dievaluasi menggunakan dua indikator utama, yaitu nilai cos θᵢ dan besar komponen proyeksi sebagai ukuran keselarasan arah, serta besar komponen lateral sebagai ukuran deviasi . Hasil analisis menunjukkan bahwa pendekatan ini mampu mengidentifikasi rute-rute yang memiliki deviasi arah besar, termasuk rute yang tidak selaras bahkan berlawanan dengan arah aman, sehingga berpotensi kurang efektif secara geometris.

title: Spectral Analysis and Structural Dynamics: The Role of Eigenvalues in Spacecraft Launch Stability
abstract: The structural integrity and stability of launch vehicles during the atmospheric ascent phase are critical factors in the success of space exploration missions. Due to their necessarily low structural mass and high flexibility, large boosters are susceptib le to complex dynamic phenomena, including aeroelastic coupling and self -excited vibrations such as "Pogo" oscillations. This paper analyzes NASA’s methodology in employing spectral analysis — specifically the derivation of eigenvalues and eigenvectors — to pr edict and reduce the risks . Eigenvalues are used to identify the natural frequencies and damping ratios of the vehicle, while eigenvectors define the physical mode shapes of the structure. Through the integration of these parameters into high-fidelity linear state -space models, NASA engineers can simulat e the interaction between rigid -body dynamics, propellant slosh, and structural vibrations. This study examines historical case studies, such as the Pogo instability encountered during the Apollo 13 mission, and modern applications in the Ares I -X pathfind er vehicle, where the Eigensystem Realization Algorithm (ERA) was employed for in-flight modal identification. Furthermore, the paper discusses the use of the NASTRAN (NASA Structural Analysis) software and the implementation of ground vibration testing (GVT) to validate Finite Element Models (FEM). The results demonstrate that precise spectral characterization all ows for the strategic separation of structural and propulsion frequencies, the optimization of damping mechanisms, and the insurance of closed -loop stability. Ultimately, the application of these algebraic frameworks transforms complex physical oscillations into predictable mathematical models, ensuring the safety of both crew and payload in extreme flight environments.

title: Penerapan Operasi Penjumlahan Vektor dalam Sistem Pergerakan Objek pada Game Snake
abstract: Perkembangan pembuatan game sangat bergantung pada logika matematika, khususnya Aljabar Linear sebagai pondasi dalam memanipulasi objek grafi s. Salah satu implementasinya adalah mekanisme pergerakan objek dalam ruang dua dimensi (2D). Makalah ini membahas penerapan operasi vektor sebagai algoritma dalam pergerakan karakter dari game klasik Snake. Metode yang digunakan adalah merepresentasikan a rea permainan sebagai sistem koordinat Kartesius, di mana posisi kepala dan setiap segmen tubuh ular didefinisikan sebagai vektor posisi (x, y). Pergerakan karakter disimulasikan melalui operasi translasi, yaitu penjumlahan antara vektor posisi saat ini de ngan vektor arah yang ditentukan oleh input pengguna. Selain itu, makalah ini juga membahas logika pergeseran vektor untuk menangani pergerakan segmen tubuh ular yang mengikuti jejak kepala.

title: A Linear Algebra Perspective on Voice Assistant Pipelines Using Singular Value Decomposition
abstract: Voice Assistants, such as Apple’s Siri and Amazon’s Alexa, plays a huge role in assisting daily digital tasks during the current era. However, deploying these systems itself poses a significant computational challenge due to high -dimensional matrix operations in audio processing and neural network inference, resulting in excessive memory usage and latency, especially on edge devices . This paper explores how Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) and low - rank matrix approximation optimize voice assistant pipelines or processes across three critical components . These components are spectrogram generation, feature extraction, and neural network model parameters. Using a simple program, we demonstrate a simple practical implementation of SVD -based techniques on Spectrogram Generation and evaluate their performance through compression ratio, relative error, and energy retention metrics.

title: Penerapan Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) dan Principal
abstract: Data yang memiliki dimensi tinggi seringkali mengandung redudansi yang menurunkan kinerja model pembelajaran mesin. Oleh karena itu penelitian ini membahas SVD sebagai pereduksi dimensi pada data numerik. Metode SVD digunakan untuk mendekomposisi matriks data jadi komponen orthogonal dengan mempertahankan singular value terbesar. Pendekatan ini mampu menekan noise sambil mempertahankan struktur utama. Dengan demikian, SVD dapat menjadi preprocessing yang efektif dalam peningkatan kualitas data terutama dalam pembelajaran mesin .

title: Annotasi Adaptif Patch Citra Apusan Dahak Bakteri Tahan Asam Berbasis Nilai Eigen Laplacian Graf
abstract: Anotasi akurat pada citra apusan dahak bakteri tahan asam (BTA) masih menjadi tantangan karena adanya noise, objek yang terfragmentasi, dan variabilitas kualitas pewarnaan pada tingkat patch. Studi ini mengusulkan kerangka kerja anotasi berbasis graf adaptif eigen yang menyempurnakan hasil segmentasi biner menggunakan sifat geometri Laplacian. Anotasi biner pertama kali dihasilkan menggunakan operator logika (RGB, OR, AND, XOR, dan XNOR), kemudian ditransformasikan menjadi graf tingkat piksel dengan beberapa skema konektivitas. Langkah pascapemrosesan adaptif diperkenalkan, di mana nilai eigen Laplacian t erkecil kedua (λ₂) dan jumlah nilai eigen nol digunakan untuk menilai konektivitas graf dan menentukan penyempurnaan iteratif serta strategi penghubungan. Evaluasi eksperimental pada citra patch BTA menunjukkan bahwa mekanisme adaptif eigen yang diusulkan secara konsisten meningkatkan kualitas segmentasi di semua operator logika. Baik penilaian visual kualitatif maupun analisis kuantitatif menggunakan metrik Intersection over Union (IoU) mengkonfirmasi bahwa metode M -AND mencapai perilaku yang paling stabil, konvergen dalam satu iterasi di semua sampel dengan menghasilkan annotasi yang secara visual paling dekat dengan ground truth-nya. Secara kuantitatif, M-AND mencapai IoU rata-rata tertinggi (0,74), yang sesuai dengan peningkatan relatif 23 .33 % dibandingkan dengan metode dasarnya. Hasil ini menunjukkan bahwa mengintegrasikan operator logika sederhana dengan kriteria geometri graf menghasilkan strategi annotasi yang efisien, mudah diinterpretasikan, dan kuat. Pendekatan yang diusulkan menawarkan alternatif ringan untuk pemrosesan adaptif dalam analisis citra mikroskopis dan sangat cocok untuk alur kerja annotasi unsupervise dalam aplikasi skrining tuberkulosis.

title: Application of SVD for Image-to-Video Search and Understanding Its Limitations
abstract: This paper presents an application of Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) for image-to-video search, where a query image is matched against frames extracted from a set of videos. Each frame is converted into a grayscale vector representation and reduced in dimensionality using SVD-based Principal Component Analysis (PCA). Similarity between the query and video frames is computed using cosine similarity in the reduced feature space. The method provides efficient data retrieval speed and preserves important structural information of the images. However, SVD-based search has several limitations, including sensitivity to illumination changes, dependence on grayscale intensity distributions, and the linearity assumption that restricts its ability to model complex visual variations. This study highlights both the practical strengths and fundamental drawbacks of SVD-based search applications in content-based video retrieval.

title: Analisis Perubahan Pola Leksikal pada Lirik Lagu Populer di Amerika Serikat (1960–2025) Menggunakan Singular Value Decomposition (SVD)
abstract: Studi ini menganalisis perubahan pola leksikal pada lirik lagu populer di Amerika Serikat dari tahun 1960 hingga 2025 . SVD digunakan untuk mengidentifikasi struktur laten yang merepresentasikan tren leksikal dominan serta perubahannya tiap tahun. Melalui analisis nilai singular dan vektor singular, penelitian ini mengidentifikasi pergeseran penggunaan kosakata, tema, dan gaya bahasa yang mencerminkan perubahan sosial budaya seiring zaman. Penelitian ini juga menunjukkan bahwa SVD merupakan pendekatan aljabar lin ear yang efektif untuk menganalisis perubahan pola leksikal dalam data teks berskala besar.

title: Euclidean Vector Application for Representing Character Attack Direction In Genshin Impact
abstract: Euclidean Vector Application for Representing Character Attack Direction In Genshin Impact

title: Application of Truncated SVD on Storage of Positional Data
abstract: SVD, or Singular Valu e Decomposition is a technique to factorize an 𝒎 × 𝒏 matrix M into the form UΣVT where U is a n 𝒎 × 𝒎 orthogonal matrix, Σ is a n 𝒎 × 𝒏 diagonal matrix containing the singular values of M , and V T is an 𝒏 × 𝒏 orthogonal matrix. Truncated SVD only takes the largest k singular values creating the 𝒎 × 𝒌 matrix U t, the 𝒌 × 𝒌 matrix Σt, and the 𝒌 × 𝒏 matrix VtT, which form a low rank approximation of M. Truncated SVD is used in image compression, noise reduction, text feature extraction, and recommendation systems. This paper examines the application of truncated SVD on sets of position and velocity vectors and comparing its accuracy to the application of truncated SVD on image compression. An implementation is provided which uses turtle graphics to compare the low rank approximation with the original matrix.

title: Application of Linear Diophantine Systems for Analyzing and Designing Crafting Trees in Video Games Billy Ontoseno Irawan
abstract: The design of complex resource crafting systems is a critical and often challenging aspect of balancing in many video games. Inefficient or exploitable crafting systems can lead to player frustration, broken economies, and degraded gameplay. This paper proposes a formal methodology for the analysis and design of crafting trees by modeling them as Linear Diophantine Systems. The proposed framework allows for the automated solution of key design problems such as identifying optimal resource gathering paths, detecting unintended resource exploits or bottlenecks, calculating precise material costs, and balancing the relative value of crafted items. The proposed methodology not only facilitates the creation of more robust and engaging crafting experiences but also substantially reduces the reliance on iterative playtesting during the development cycle.

student.name: School Of Electrical Engineering And Informatics

title: Comparative Analysis of Face Recognition: Eigenfaces (PCA) vs Fisherfaces (LDA) under Varying Facial Expressions and Illumination
abstract: Face recognition is one of the most widely used biometric technologies in computer vision. However, the accuracy of recognition systems often degrades significantly under uncontrolled environments, particularly due to varying illumination conditions and facial expressions. This paper presents a comparative analysis between two fundamental linear algebrabased algorithms: Eigenfaces, which utilizes Principal Component Analysis (PCA), and Fisherfaces, which utilizes Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA). The Eigenfaces method reduces dimensionality by calculating the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the covariance matrix to maximize total variance. In contrast, the Fisherfaces method seeks to maximize the ratio of betweenclass scatter to within-class scatter matrices to find the most discriminative features.
